revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/12680 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.fukuoka-bunkazai.jp/frmDetail.aspx?db=1&id=69 Cultural Properties in Fukuoka Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]

The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sekijinsan Kofun | native_name =石神山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sekijinsan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyama, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|05|39|N|130|28|46|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Sekijinsan Kofun'''|石神山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Takatamachi Kamikusuda neighborhood of the city of [[Miyama, Fukuoka|Miyama]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173275|title=石神山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref> It should not be confused with the {{nihongo|Sekijinsan Kofun|石人山古墳}} located in [[Hirokawa, Fukuoka]], whose name is written in slightly different ''[[kanji]]''.
==Overview== The Sekijinsan Kofun is located at the tip of a hill that extends from east-to-west to the north of Kamikusuda hamlet. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 58.5 meters, and constructed in three tiers. It is orientated to the east, and has a posterior circular portion diameter of 18 meters and a usually low and narrow anterior rectangular portion of six meters in with and eight meters in length. There is a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' on the top of the hill. In 1911, while local people were clearing land in the area, three boat-shaped [[sarcophagi]] were discovered in the center of the posterior circle. All of the sarcophagi are made of [[tuff]] from [[Mount Aso]], with the insides painted in vermilion, and each one is of a different size. The large and medium-sized sarcophagi align with the main axis, while the small coffin is located to the west and perpendicular to the main axis. The largest sarcophagus has protrusions for ropes (two on each side), where as the smaller ones had one on the shorter side and two on the longer side. [[Grave goods]] excavated include iron sword pieces, iron dagger pieces, a bronze chime, and silver-plated copperware. No trace of a stone [[burial chamber]] has been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus also has a 107-cm tall stone figure at the top of the posteior circle, also made of tuff from Mount Aso. It depicting an armed stone figure with a helmet and short armor, originally painted with red pigment. The image is weathered, and is missing its legs, although triangular decorations on the [[cuirass]] are well-preserved. Such stone figures are a characteristic of ''kofun'' primarily in northern Kyushu, and date from the 5th to 6th centuries. In addition to armored warriors, animals such as horses, boars and chickens are commonly found. Such depictions are also found in figurative ''[[haniwa]]'' and it is thought that these stone images were used in lieu of ''haniwa'' in this region of Kyushu. The statue was designed a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213209|title=武装石人|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is located behind the Kamikusuda Tenman-gū shrine. A shed has been installed to protect the statue and the three sarcophagi; although not normally open to visitors, it is possible to view these artifacts through a window. The site is approximately a 30-minute walk from [[Wataze Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]]
